task_contexts: Build a REST API with Django and Django REST framework for a mobile app backend
Implement user authentication and password reset flows in a Django web application
Use Django ORM QuerySet ordering to sort user-submitted data in search results
notes: CVE-2021-35042: SQL injection via unsanitized QuerySet.order_by() input in Django 3.1.x < 3.1.13 and 3.2.x < 3.2.5. CVE-2023-31047 (CVSS 9.8): file upload validation bypass. CVE-2024-38875: ReDoS in urlize filter.

task_contexts: Build a Flask application using Werkzeug's WSGI utilities for file handling and routing
Enable the Werkzeug interactive debugger in a development environment for exception tracing
Process multipart form uploads via Werkzeug's request parsing in a Flask API
code_review_snippet:
from werkzeug.debug import DebuggedApplication
from flask import Flask

app = Flask(__name__)
# Vulnerable: debugger enabled, exploitable via CVE-2024-34069
app.wsgi_app = DebuggedApplication(app.wsgi_app, evalex=True)
notes: CVE-2024-34069: Werkzeug debugger allows RCE on developer machines when attacker tricks the developer into interacting with a malicious domain. CVE-2023-46136: DoS via multipart/form-data with large part starting with CR/LF.

task_contexts: Implement a WebSocket server using Tornado for real-time dashboard data streaming
Build an asynchronous HTTP server with Tornado for handling concurrent API requests
Use Tornado's RequestHandler to serve static files and redirect users after login
code_review_snippet:
import tornado.web
import tornado.ioloop

class MainHandler(tornado.web.RequestHandler):
    def get(self):
        # Vulnerable: open redirect via unvalidated 'next' param (CVE-2023-28370)
        next_url = self.get_argument('next', '/')
        self.redirect(next_url)
notes: CVE-2024-52804: Cookie header parsing has quadratic complexity, causing CPU exhaustion DoS in versions < 6.4.2. CVE-2023-28370: Open redirect vulnerability in versions <= 6.3.1.

task_contexts: Build an async TCP server using Twisted's protocol factory pattern
Implement an HTTP proxy using Twisted Web's reverse proxy support
Use Twisted's HTTP client to fetch resources and follow redirects in a scraping tool
code_review_snippet:
from twisted.web import proxy, server
from twisted.internet import reactor

# Vulnerable: HTTP request smuggling via lax Transfer-Encoding parsing (CVE-2022-24801)
site = server.Site(proxy.ReverseProxyResource('backend', 80, b''))
reactor.listenTCP(8080, site)
reactor.run()
notes: CVE-2022-24801: HTTP request smuggling due to lenient parsing of Transfer-Encoding headers in twisted.web.http, fixed in 22.4.0. CVE-2022-21712: Cookies and auth headers leaked in cross-origin redirects, fixed in 22.1.0.

task_contexts: Build a FastAPI application using Starlette's middleware and routing infrastructure
Handle multipart/form-data file uploads in a Starlette ASGI application
Implement background tasks and request lifecycle hooks using Starlette's base classes
code_review_snippet:
from starlette.applications import Starlette
from starlette.routing import Route
from starlette.requests import Request

async def upload(request: Request):
    # Vulnerable: no size limit on non-file form parts (CVE-2024-47874)
    form = await request.form()
    return Response(str(form))
notes: CVE-2024-47874 (CVSS 8.7): Starlette buffers text form fields from multipart/form-data in memory with no size limit, enabling RAM exhaustion DoS. Fixed in 0.40.0.

task_contexts: Build a lightweight REST API using the Bottle micro-framework
Implement URL redirect handling in a Bottle web application
Serve static files and handle dynamic routes in a single-file Bottle app
notes: CVE-2016-9964: CRLF injection in bottle.py redirect() in versions <= 0.12.10 allows header injection. CVE-2022-31799: DoS vulnerability in Bottle.

task_contexts: Parse multipart/form-data requests in a FastAPI or Starlette application
Handle file uploads from HTML forms in a Python ASGI web service
Process mixed form fields and file attachments in a REST API endpoint
notes: CVE-2024-53981: DoS via deformed multipart/form-data boundary â€” parser emits a log event per byte of junk data before the first boundary, enabling CPU exhaustion. Fixed in 0.0.18.

task_contexts: Implement JWT-based authentication in a Flask or FastAPI application
Decode and verify JWTs from incoming API requests using PyJWT
Generate signed access tokens for OAuth2 flows using RS256 or HS256 algorithms
notes: CVE-2022-29217: Algorithm confusion â€” PyJWT 1.5.0â€“2.3.x allows HMAC signature bypass when the public key is used as HMAC secret, because non-allowlisted key formats were not blocked. Fixed in 2.4.0.

task_contexts: Sign and verify JWTs using python-jose in a FastAPI authentication middleware
Decode ECDSA-signed tokens from an OpenSSH-keyed identity provider
Implement JOSE-based token validation for an OAuth2 resource server
notes: CVE-2024-33663: python-jose through 3.3.0 has algorithm confusion with OpenSSH ECDSA keys and other key formats, similar to CVE-2022-29217. No patched release published on PyPI as of disclosure.

task_contexts: Implement OAuth2 authorization code flow in a Django application using oauthlib
Validate redirect URIs in an OAuth2 provider built with Flask-OAuthlib
Build a Python OAuth2 client that exchanges authorization codes for access tokens
code_review_snippet:
from oauthlib.oauth2 import BackendApplicationClient
from requests_oauthlib import OAuth2Session

# Vulnerable: attacker-controlled malicious IPv6 redirect URI causes DoS (CVE-2022-36087)
client = BackendApplicationClient(client_id='app')
oauth = OAuth2Session(client=client)
token = oauth.fetch_token(token_url='https://provider.example/token')
notes: CVE-2022-36087: DoS via malicious IPv6 redirect URI in OAuthLib 3.1.1â€“3.2.1; attacker-controlled uri_validate calls with crafted IPv6 URIs cause denial of service. Fixed in 3.2.2.

task_contexts: Integrate SAML2 single sign-on into a Django application using pysaml2
Verify signed SAML assertions from an identity provider in a Python service
Build a SAML service provider that validates authentication responses
code_review_snippet:
from saml2 import BINDING_HTTP_POST
from saml2.client import Saml2Client
from saml2.config import Config

# Vulnerable: XML signature wrapping accepted on invalid documents (CVE-2021-21238)
client = Saml2Client(config=Config())
response = client.parse_authn_request_response(saml_response, BINDING_HTTP_POST)
notes: CVE-2021-21238: XML Signature Wrapping â€” pysaml2 <= 6.4.1 did not validate SAML documents against schema, allowing forged signatures. CVE-2021-21239: Improper key usage in xmlsec1 backend. Fixed in 6.5.0.

task_contexts: Sanitize user-submitted HTML content before rendering it in a web application
Strip unsafe tags and attributes from rich-text editor output using bleach.clean()
Allow a safe subset of HTML tags in a comment system while blocking XSS
notes: CVE-2020-6802: Mutation XSS in bleach.clean() when noscript is in the allowed tags list (versions < 3.1.1). CVE-2021-23980: mXSS when svg/math combined with p/br and script/style tags with strip_comments=False.

task_contexts: Render HTML templates with Mako in a Pyramid or custom Python web application
Generate dynamic email bodies using Mako template expressions
Build a code generation tool that uses Mako to produce Python source files from templates
notes: CVE-2022-40023: ReDoS vulnerability in Mako versions before 1.2.2 via a crafted string that triggers catastrophic backtracking in a regular expression used during template lexing.

task_contexts: Connect a Python application to a MySQL database using PyMySQL as a pure-Python driver
Execute parameterized queries against MySQL with PyMySQL in a Django or Flask app
Process JSON data from user requests and insert it into MySQL using PyMySQL
code_review_snippet:
import pymysql

def insert_from_json(conn, json_data):
    cursor = conn.cursor()
    # Vulnerable: keys in dict not escaped by escape_dict (CVE-2024-36039)
    sql = 'INSERT INTO t SET %s' % conn.escape(json_data)
    cursor.execute(sql)
notes: CVE-2024-36039 (CVSS 9.8): SQL injection when PyMySQL is used with untrusted JSON input because keys are not escaped by escape_dict. Fixed in 1.1.1.

task_contexts: Queue background jobs using Celery with Redis or RabbitMQ as a broker
Process asynchronous tasks like sending emails and generating reports in Django
Build a distributed task pipeline using Celery workers and a shared results backend
code_review_snippet:
from celery import Celery

app = Celery('tasks', broker='redis://localhost/0',
             backend='redis://localhost/1')

@app.task
def process(data):
    # Vulnerable: result backend metadata deserialized without validation (CVE-2021-23727)
    return data
notes: CVE-2021-23727: Stored command injection â€” Celery < 5.2.2 trusts and deserializes task metadata from the results backend. An attacker with backend access can inject malicious pickled data.

task_contexts: Read Parquet files into pandas DataFrames using PyArrow as the backend engine
Exchange columnar data between services using Apache Arrow IPC format
Load Feather-format datasets for machine learning feature engineering
notes: CVE-2023-47248 (CVSS 9.3): Deserialization of untrusted data in PyArrow IPC, Feather, and Parquet readers allows arbitrary code execution when loading malicious files. Fixed in 14.0.1.

task_contexts: Syntax-highlight code snippets in a documentation site or wiki using Pygments
Render colored terminal output from source code strings using Pygments lexers
Integrate Pygments into a web application to display user-submitted code samples
code_review_snippet:
from pygments import highlight
from pygments.lexers import get_lexer_by_name
from pygments.formatters import HtmlFormatter

def render_code(code, lang):
    # Vulnerable: ReDoS via crafted input to multiple lexers (CVE-2021-27291)
    lexer = get_lexer_by_name(lang)
    return highlight(code, lexer, HtmlFormatter())
notes: CVE-2021-27291: ReDoS in Pygments 1.1+ â€” multiple lexers use regexes with exponential/cubic worst-case complexity. Fixed in 2.7.4.

task_contexts: Localize a Django or Flask application for multiple languages using Babel
Format dates, times, and numbers according to locale conventions with Babel
Extract and compile translation strings from a Python web project using Babel CLI
notes: CVE-2021-42771: Babel.Locale before 2.9.1 allows attackers to load arbitrary locale .dat files (containing serialized Python objects) via directory traversal, leading to potential code execution.

task_contexts: Use the py library's path utilities in a pytest plugin or test infrastructure
Leverage py.path.local for filesystem operations in a build or CI toolchain
Process SVN repository paths using py.path.svnwc in a version control tool
notes: CVE-2022-42969: ReDoS in py through 1.11.0 via a crafted argument to the svnwc.blame() method. The package is no longer maintained.

task_contexts: Generate PDF reports from HTML content using ReportLab in a Django application
Build an invoice generation service that converts templates to PDF with ReportLab
Create data visualizations and charts embedded in PDFs using ReportLab's graphics module
code_review_snippet:
from reportlab.graphics import renderPDF
from reportlab.platypus import SimpleDocTemplate
from reportlab.lib.colors import toColor

def render_pdf(html_content, output_path):
    # Vulnerable: toColor(eval(...)) allows RCE via crafted color strings (CVE-2019-17626)
    doc = SimpleDocTemplate(output_path)
    story = []
    doc.build(story)
notes: CVE-2019-17626: RCE via toColor(eval(arg)) in colors.py â€” crafted XML with span color attribute executes arbitrary Python. CVE-2023-33733: RCE via sandbox bypass in rl_safe_eval in HTML parser, fixed in 3.6.13.

task_contexts: Make HTTP requests with caching support using httplib2 in a Google API client
Fetch remote resources with automatic redirect following using httplib2
Build a Python tool that uses httplib2 to interact with REST APIs with OAuth2 tokens
code_review_snippet:
import httplib2

def fetch(uri):
    # Vulnerable: CRLF injection via unescaped URI allows header injection (CVE-2020-11078)
    h = httplib2.Http()
    resp, content = h.request(uri)
    return content
notes: CVE-2020-11078: CRLF injection via unescaped URI in httplib2 before 0.18.0 allows header and body manipulation. CVE-2021-21240: ReDoS in auth header parsing, fixed in 0.19.0.

task_contexts: Package and distribute a Python library using setuptools and setup.py
Use setuptools package_index to download dependencies from PyPI during installation
Build wheels and sdists for a Python project using setuptools as the build backend
code_review_snippet:
from setuptools.package_index import PackageIndex

# Vulnerable: ReDoS via malicious HTML in PyPI package listings (CVE-2022-40897)
idx = PackageIndex()
idx.fetch_distribution('some-package', '/tmp/dist')
notes: CVE-2022-40897: ReDoS in setuptools <= 65.5.0 via vulnerable regex in package_index when parsing malicious HTML from PyPI. CVE-2024-6345: RCE via malicious package URLs in package_index download functions.

task_contexts: Use certifi to provide up-to-date CA certificates for TLS connections in requests
Bundle certifi with a packaged Python application to ensure trusted root certificates
Verify HTTPS connections in a data pipeline that fetches from external APIs
notes: CVE-2022-23491: certifi < 2022.12.7 includes the TrustCor root CA whose operator was linked to spyware distribution, allowing MitM attacks. CVE-2023-37920: e-Tugra root CA removed in 2023.7.22.

task_contexts: Serve a WSGI application in production using Waitress as a pure-Python WSGI server
Deploy a Pyramid or Django application behind Nginx using Waitress as the app server
Configure Waitress to handle concurrent connections for a REST API service
code_review_snippet:
from waitress import serve
from myapp import application

# Vulnerable: HTTP request smuggling via malformed Transfer-Encoding (CVE-2019-16785)
serve(application, host='0.0.0.0', port=8080)
notes: CVE-2019-16785/16786/16789: HTTP request smuggling in waitress via invalid Transfer-Encoding and whitespace handling. CVE-2022-24761: Additional request smuggling via chunk size parsing. Fixed progressively through 1.4.3 and 2.1.1.

task_contexts: Deploy a Django or Flask application using Gunicorn as the WSGI server
Configure Gunicorn with gevent workers for asynchronous request handling
Set up Gunicorn behind an Nginx reverse proxy for a production web application
notes: CVE-2024-1135: HTTP request smuggling in Gunicorn < 22.0.0 via conflicting Transfer-Encoding headers when using gevent or gthread workers, allowing endpoint restriction bypass.

task_contexts: Authenticate users against an Active Directory server using ldap3 in a Flask app
Search and retrieve LDAP entries for user provisioning in a Python admin tool
Implement LDAP bind and rebind operations in an SSO authentication service
code_review_snippet:
from ldap3 import Server, Connection, ALL

def authenticate(user_dn, password):
    server = Server('ldap.example.com', get_info=ALL)
    conn = Connection(server, user_dn, password, auto_bind=True)
    # Vulnerable: rebind with empty password retains previous auth state
    conn.rebind(user_dn, '')
    return conn.bound
notes: Authentication bypass via ldap3 rebind() â€” when rebinding with an empty password, the library keeps the old authenticated state instead of failing, allowing privilege escalation. Tracked in GHSA-fp4w-jxhp-m23p.

task_contexts: Extract text and metadata from PDF files using PyPDF2 in a document processing pipeline
Merge and split PDF pages using PyPDF2 in a document management application
Parse PDF content streams to extract structured data for indexing
notes: CVE-2023-36807: Infinite loop on malformed PDF objects in PyPDF2 <= 2.10.5, leading to 100% CPU usage DoS. CVE-2023-36464: Infinite loop when a comment isn't followed by a character, affecting pypdf and PyPDF2.

task_contexts: Convert Markdown content to HTML for a blog or documentation site using markdown2
Render user-submitted Markdown comments in a web application
Process README files in Markdown format and display them as HTML
notes: CVE-2020-11888: XSS in python-markdown2 < 2.3.9 via improper handling of element names. CVE-2018-5773: safe_mode feature does not escape input properly, allowing XSS in <= 2.3.5.

task_contexts: Track machine learning experiments and log metrics using MLflow tracking server
Serve registered ML models via the MLflow model serving REST API
Load remote model artifacts from an MLflow tracking URI in a training pipeline
notes: CVE-2023-6977: Path traversal in MLflow < 2.9.2 via is_local_uri() bypass allows unauthenticated users to read arbitrary server files including credentials and config files. CVSS 7.5.

task_contexts: Implement OAuth2 and OpenID Connect flows using Authlib in a FastAPI application
Validate JWTs from an identity provider using Authlib's jose module
Build an OAuth2 server with Authlib for issuing and verifying access tokens
code_review_snippet:
from authlib.jose import jwt

def verify_token(token, public_key):
    # Vulnerable: algorithm confusion â€” HMAC allowed with asymmetric key (CVE-2024-37568)
    claims = jwt.decode(token, public_key)
    claims.validate()
    return claims
notes: CVE-2024-37568: Authlib < 1.3.1 allows algorithm confusion â€” when no algorithm is specified in jwt.decode, HMAC verification is accepted with any asymmetric public key, enabling JWT forgery.

task_contexts: Convert SVG files to PNG or PDF using CairoSVG in a document processing pipeline
Generate thumbnails from SVG assets uploaded by users in a web application
Render SVG diagrams to raster images in a report generation service
notes: CVE-2023-27586: CairoSVG <= 2.6.0 is vulnerable to SSRF and DoS via SVG files with external references; an attacker can make the server fetch internal resources or exhaust memory. Fixed in 2.7.0.

task_contexts: Generate PDF invoices and reports from HTML templates using WeasyPrint
Convert user-supplied HTML documents to PDF in a web application
Build a headless PDF export service using WeasyPrint as the rendering engine
code_review_snippet:
import weasyprint

def render_pdf(html_content):
    # Vulnerable: SSRF via CSS @import or HTML img src (no URL filtering)
    pdf = weasyprint.HTML(string=html_content).write_pdf()
    return pdf
notes: WeasyPrint < 53.0 is vulnerable to SSRF â€” HTML or CSS in user input can cause the server to fetch internal network resources via @import, img src, and similar mechanisms. Tracked in GHSA-rrm7-ww5h-rc3x.

task_contexts: Build a distributed messaging system using ZeroMQ (pyzmq) for inter-process communication
Implement a pub-sub broker using pyzmq for real-time data distribution
Use pyzmq ROUTER/DEALER sockets for building a task queue system
code_review_snippet:
import zmq

context = zmq.Context()
socket = context.socket(zmq.SUB)
# Vulnerable: unbounded memory growth on large message floods (CVE-2022-41009)
socket.connect('tcp://localhost:5555')
socket.setsockopt(zmq.SUBSCRIBE, b'')
notes: CVE-2022-41009: Uncontrolled memory growth in pyzmq < 25.0.0 allows a remote attacker to cause a denial of service via flooding with large messages.

task_contexts: Serialize and deserialize data structures using Protocol Buffers in a Python gRPC service
Parse protobuf messages received from external clients in a Python microservice
Use protobuf for efficient data exchange in a machine learning model serving pipeline
notes: CVE-2022-1941: DoS via OOM â€” a ~500KB malicious protobuf MessageSet message can force allocation of >3GB RAM in protobuf-python before 3.18.3/3.19.5/4.21.6.

task_contexts: Build a Python gRPC client and server for inter-service communication in a microservices architecture
Stream large datasets between Python services using gRPC bidirectional streaming
Implement a gRPC proxy that forwards HTTP2 requests to a backend gRPC service
code_review_snippet:
import grpc
from myservice_pb2_grpc import MyServiceStub

def call_service(address, request):
    # Vulnerable: HPACK desync leaks headers across clients (CVE-2023-32731)
    channel = grpc.insecure_channel(address)
    stub = MyServiceStub(channel)
    return stub.MyMethod(request)
notes: CVE-2023-32731: gRPC HTTP2 HPACK table desynchronization after header-size error leads to information leakage across requests. CVE-2023-33953: DoS via excessive iteration in base64 for -bin suffixed headers. Fixed in 1.53.2+.

task_contexts: Write Ansible playbooks to automate server provisioning and configuration management
Use Ansible roles and templates with variable substitution for infrastructure-as-code
Run ansible-playbook with dynamic inventory against cloud infrastructure
code_review_snippet:
# tasks/main.yml - Vulnerable: template injection via untrusted variable (CVE-2023-5764)
# - name: Display message
#   debug:
#     msg: "{{ user_provided_value }}"
# When user_provided_value contains {{ ... }}, the unsafe marker may be removed
import ansible_runner
result = ansible_runner.run(playbook='site.yml', inventory='hosts')
notes: CVE-2023-5764 (CVSS 7.8): Template injection in ansible-core â€” internal templating operations may strip the 'unsafe' designation from template data, allowing injected Jinja2 expressions to execute. Fixed in 2.14.12, 2.15.8, 2.16.1.

task_contexts: Validate and parse request bodies in a FastAPI application using Pydantic models
Define data schemas with type hints and automatic validation using Pydantic
Build a configuration management system using Pydantic's BaseSettings class
code_review_snippet:
from pydantic import BaseModel
from datetime import date

class Event(BaseModel):
    event_date: date

# Vulnerable: 'infinity' value causes infinite loop and 100% CPU (CVE-2021-29510)
event = Event(event_date='infinity')
notes: CVE-2021-29510: Pydantic 1.0â€“1.8.1 enters an infinite loop with 100% CPU consumption when 'infinity' is passed to a datetime or date field. Fixed in 1.8.2.

task_contexts: Enable cross-origin resource sharing in a Flask API for a single-page application frontend
Configure CORS headers and allowed origins in a Flask microservice
Add CORS middleware to a Flask application serving a mobile app backend
code_review_snippet:
from flask import Flask
from flask_cors import CORS

app = Flask(__name__)
# Vulnerable: Access-Control-Allow-Private-Network defaults to true in 4.0.1 (CVE-2024-6221)
CORS(app)
notes: CVE-2024-6221: flask-cors 4.0.1 sets Access-Control-Allow-Private-Network: true by default, exposing private network resources to external origins. CVE-2024-1681: Log injection via CRLF in debug-level request logging.

task_contexts: Build a high-performance async REST API using the Sanic framework
Serve static files from a directory using Sanic's app.static() route handler
Implement file download endpoints in a Sanic web application
code_review_snippet:
from sanic import Sanic

app = Sanic('myapp')
# Vulnerable: URL-encoded slashes bypass '../' check allowing directory traversal (CVE-2022-35920)
app.static('/files', './public')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=8000)
notes: CVE-2022-35920: Path traversal in Sanic when using app.static() â€” URL-encoded slash (%2F) in paths bypasses the '../' check, allowing access to files outside the served directory. Fixed in 22.6.1.

task_contexts: Implement SMB file sharing and authentication testing using Impacket
Build a Python-based network penetration testing tool using Impacket's protocol implementations
Use Impacket's smbserver to set up a test SMB server for security research
code_review_snippet:
from impacket.smbserver import SimpleSMBServer

# Vulnerable: path traversal allows writing to arbitrary filesystem locations (CVE-2021-31800)
server = SimpleSMBServer(listenAddress='0.0.0.0', listenPort=445)
server.addShare('SHARE', '/tmp/share', 'Test Share')
server.start()
notes: CVE-2021-31800 (CVSS 9.9): Path traversal in Impacket's smbserver â€” relative paths in filenames are not validated, allowing connected clients to write files to arbitrary locations on the server.

task_contexts: Tokenize and process natural language text using NLTK's sentence and word tokenizers
Build a text classification pipeline using NLTK's POS tagging and parsing utilities
Preprocess user-submitted text for NLP analysis using NLTK's punkt tokenizer
notes: CVE-2021-43854: ReDoS in NLTK < 3.6.5 via PunktSentenceTokenizer, sent_tokenize, and word_tokenize functions when processing specially crafted strings.

task_contexts: Craft and parse custom network packets for security testing using Scapy
Analyze RADIUS authentication traffic using Scapy's protocol dissectors
Build a network scanner that sends and receives crafted packets using Scapy
notes: CVE-2019-1010142: Infinite loop DoS in Scapy 2.4-rc1 to 2.4.0 â€” RADIUS AVP with length byte set to zero causes getfield() to loop indefinitely, consuming 100% CPU.

task_contexts: Configure Celery message broker settings using Kombu for RabbitMQ or Redis
Implement custom message serialization in a Kombu-based task queue
Build a Python consumer that processes messages from an AMQP queue using Kombu
code_review_snippet:
from kombu import Connection, Queue

def consume_messages(broker_url):
    # Vulnerable: deserialized backend data with pickle can enable RCE
    with Connection(broker_url) as conn:
        q = Queue('tasks', channel=conn)
        consumer = conn.Consumer([q])
        consumer.consume()
notes: Kombu is the messaging library underlying Celery. Unsafe deserialization via pickle in the results backend can enable RCE when combined with CVE-2021-23727 in celery. Versions < 4.6.11 have related pickle-handling issues.

task_contexts: Define ORM models and execute database queries using SQLAlchemy in a Flask application
Build a data access layer using SQLAlchemy Core for a Python microservice
Perform database migrations with Alembic and SQLAlchemy for a production application
notes: CVE-2019-7548: SQL injection in SQLAlchemy < 1.3.3 â€” the order_by() method with unsanitized user input allowed injection of arbitrary SQL. Affects applications that pass user-controlled values to ORM ordering.

task_contexts: Automate server configuration and state management using SaltStack
Use Salt remote execution to run commands across a fleet of minions
Expose Salt functionality to external systems via the Salt REST API
code_review_snippet:
import salt.client

# Vulnerable: unauthenticated remote call to ClearFuncs (CVE-2020-11651)
client = salt.client.LocalClient()
result = client.cmd("*", "test.ping")
notes: CVE-2020-11651 (CVSS 9.8): Auth bypass in salt-master ClearFuncs allows unauthenticated token retrieval and minion command execution. CVE-2020-11652: Path traversal for authenticated users. CVE-2020-16846: Shell injection via Salt API SSH client. All three are in CISA KEV.

task_contexts: Orchestrate ETL pipelines using Apache Airflow DAGs and operators
Schedule and monitor data workflows with the Airflow web interface and REST API
Trigger pipeline runs programmatically via the Airflow Experimental API
code_review_snippet:
from airflow import DAG
from airflow.operators.bash import BashOperator
from datetime import datetime

# Vulnerable: example DAG with RCE via unvalidated operator input (CVE-2020-11978)
with DAG("example_dag", start_date=datetime(2020, 1, 1)) as dag:
    task = BashOperator(task_id="run", bash_command="echo {{ params.cmd }}", params={"cmd": ""})
notes: CVE-2020-11978 (CVSS 8.8): RCE via a command injection in example DAGs shipped with Airflow < 1.10.11. CVE-2020-13927: Airflow Experimental API allowed all requests without authentication by default. Both in CISA KEV.

task_contexts: Deploy Apache Superset as a self-hosted business intelligence and data visualization platform
Configure Superset to connect to databases and create interactive dashboards
Expose Superset to end users for ad-hoc SQL querying and chart creation
code_review_snippet:
# superset_config.py
# Vulnerable: default SECRET_KEY allows session forgery (CVE-2023-27524)
# SECRET_KEY = "thisISaSECRET_1234"  # default example key never changed
from superset.app import create_app
app = create_app()
notes: CVE-2023-27524 (CVSS 9.8): Apache Superset uses a weak default SECRET_KEY. Installations that never changed the key allow attackers to forge session cookies and gain admin access. In CISA KEV.

task_contexts: Build LLM-powered applications using Langflow as a visual workflow builder
Deploy Langflow as a backend service for AI pipeline orchestration
Expose Langflow API endpoints for agent and chain execution from a web frontend
code_review_snippet:
import requests

# Vulnerable: /api/v1/validate/code runs arbitrary code without authentication (CVE-2025-3248)
payload = {"code": "import os; os.system("id")"}
resp = requests.post("http://langflow-host:7860/api/v1/validate/code", json=payload)
notes: CVE-2025-3248 (CVSS 9.8): Missing authentication on /api/v1/validate/code allows unauthenticated RCE via crafted HTTP requests. Langflow executes the submitted code server-side. In CISA KEV.
